concept: Sick / Ill / The Body Out of Accord
description: Sick: the body out of its accord — fevered, weakened, unwell. The state 'nepha' (medicine), 'theala' (heal), and 'temoe' (tend) exist to answer. Its recovery-side companions: 'sunai' (health), 'wolu' (healthy).
sound_symbolism: 'mo' is the grounded body, 'ke' the catch where something has gone wrong, 'la' the settling into the state — illness as a place one is, temporarily.
grammatical_notes: Predicative: 'lopia mokela nai.' (The child is sick.) 'mia mokela to nai.' (I was sick.) The caring question: 'wa thia mokela nai.' (Are you sick?)

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: 'I am sick' is a sentence that asks for care without begging — the plain adjective dignifies the asking.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The sickbed organized the household: who tends, who fetches, who takes the field-work. Illness was communal logistics before it was diagnosis.
